entry.title: ABAP Cloud Learning Map
entry.content:
The ABAP Cloud development model (ABAP Cloud) is the foundation of ABAP-based development at SAP, its partners, and customers, designed from the ground up to adhere to clean core principles. It brings together evolving technologies and methodologies into a sustainable, long-lasting programming model. 

The ABAP Cloud development model encompasses all aspects of modern cloud-ready software development, resulting in a wide range of topics that need to be understood. Therefore, clear guidance is helpful for getting started with and navigating ABAP Cloud.

Discover the ABAP Cloud Learning Map🗺️ – your guide to mastering the ABAP Cloud development model. It provides an overview of essential topics and skills, along with a curated collection of links to key resources covering the technologies, tools, approaches, and practices that drive cloud-ready, upgrade-stable ABAP development. 📚Whether you’re starting out or filling knowledge gaps, the learning map helps you navigate modern ABAP Cloud development at your own pace. 

🔗Access it here: ABAP Cloud Learning Map↗

entry.title: Where ORD Is Used in SAP Datasphere, SAP Business Data Cloud & SAP Databricks
entry.content:
SAP Open Resource Discovery (ORD) is becoming a foundational standard across the SAP ecosystem because it provides a consistent, self‑describing metadata layer for APIs, events, business objects, and data products. This metadata standardization is what enables smooth interoperability across SAP’s modern data and integration platforms.

Below are the key places where ORD is used today — and how it empowers seamless integration, especially with SAP Datasphere, SAP Business Data Cloud, and SAP Databricks.

1. SAP Business Accelerator Hub (api.sap.com)

SAP Business Accelerator Hub uses ORD as one of the primary mechanisms for publishing APIs and events. Any API showing an ORD ID on its overview page is officially ORD-enabled, meaning it exposes standardized metadata that can be consumed by SAP tools and data platforms.

This ensures:

  • Consistent API descriptions
  • Discoverability by SAP’s central aggregators
  • Compatibility with downstream systems like SAP Business Data Cloud
This is the first indicator that an API can participate in the broader data product ecosystem. 
 

2. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)

SAP uses ORD internally to improve SAP-to-SAP integration and provide a unified metadata layer that development tools (like SAP Business Application Studio) can consume for enhanced API discovery and automation.

Each Product Owner from SAP Products LoB will be develop, design ORD to make it available in SAP Business Accelerator Hub (api.sap.com) and Data Products Marketplace for you..

3. SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC)

SAP Business Data Cloud actively uses ORD to describe Data Products and make them discoverable to consumers across the SAP ecosystem, including SAP Datasphere. ORD helps BDC deliver a unified catalog with:

  • Descriptions of datasets
  • Relationships
  • Capabilities
  • Higher-level metadata structures like data products and business entities
In addition, third‑party systems such as SAP Databricks are required to describe data products using ORD before publishing them into SAP Business Data Cloud.
 
This ensures smooth catalog integration and consistent metadata across SAP and non-SAP data sources.
 

4. SAP Datasphere

SAP Datasphere consumes data products published to SAP Business Data Cloud — and because BDC requires ORD‑based metadata, Datasphere gains:

  • Automatically discoverable data products
  • Standardized metadata for modeling
  • Compatibility with external data such as Databricks Delta Lake

Datasphere uses ORD indirectly because it relies on BDC’s ORD-driven catalog for understanding and federating data assets.

Additionally, SAP’s hybrid integration patterns show that Datasphere can integrate with Databricks using JDBC or federated models — and ORD ensures data products between these systems are consistently described.

5. SAP Databricks (Enterprise Databricks Integration)

SAP Databricks integrates deeply with SAP Business Data Cloud using BDC Connect, enabling bi-directional sharing of data products. To publish a data product from SAP Databricks to SAP Business Data Cloud:

  • The metadata must be described using ORD and CSN (Core Schema Notation)
  • The data product becomes discoverable to SAP systems (e.g., Datasphere) via BDC

Furthermore, Databricks–SAP integration uses Delta Share, JDBC, and federated models, but ORD plays the critical role of standardizing data product metadata.

This creates a smooth, unified integration path:

SAP applications → ORD-based metadata → Business Data Cloud → Datasphere & Databricks

SAP Architecture Center also confirms this hybrid pattern where business data from SAP and non‑SAP (Databricks) is harmonized through Business Data Cloud and Datasphere, enabling interoperable AI/ML and analytics scenarios.

 Refer my SAP blog

6. Data Products Across the SAP Ecosystem

ORD also provides standardized metadata for:

  • Entity Types / Business Objects
  • APIs and Events
  • Data Products 
This consistency is the key enabler for cross‑platform interoperability — especially as Data Products become central to the SAP ecosystem via Business Data Cloud and Datasphere.

Summary: How ORD Enables Smooth Integration Across SAP & Databricks

With ORD in place:

  • APIs with an ORD ID on api.sap.com can be safely consumed to build data products in SAP Business Data Cloud.
  • Business Data Cloud uses ORD metadata to catalog and serve data products to SAP Datasphere and SAP Databricks.
  • SAP Databricks uses ORD when publishing its own data products back into Business Data Cloud.
  • SAP Datasphere leverages ORD-driven metadata to discover, model, and federate data products seamlessly.

ORD is the metadata “glue” that ensures smooth integration across:

SAP BTP → Business Accelerator Hub → Business Data Cloud → Datasphere → Databricks

And the more SAP and partner solutions adopt ORD, the smoother, more automated, and more scalable integrations become across the SAP landscape.

entry.title: Demand and Supply Segmentation in SAP S/4 HANA - Segmentation Setup (Blog 1)
entry.content:
This blog series provides a fundamental understanding on Segmentation and its end-to-end integration with other capabilities such as Advanced ATP, Planning, Production, Supply Assignment, PPDS, EWM, etc. in SAP S/4 HANA Private Cloud Edition.

In this blog, we will focus on the Segmentation Master Data setup and assigning that to a material.

Background:

  • Segmentation was a feature available only for Fashion industry and due to its widespread relevancy is now accessible for all other industries too.

Why do we need Segmentation?:

  • Segmentation can serve both physical and logical needs.
  • Minimizes the number of products to be created e.g. materials with different quality grades and revisions can be set up as single material with multiple segmentation values.
  • Flexibility to share the inventory against different segmentation values using ATP Pool Segment setup and ARUN/MRP NM Segmentation setup.
  • Controls planning, production, fulfillment processes at segment level.

Basic Concepts of Segmentation:

  • Segmentation Structure
    • Defines the structure of Segmentation set up.
    • Recommended having different structures for different product categories as the characteristics and rules could be different.
    • List all the characteristics to be set up under a segmentation value (e.g. Characteristic 1 – Country of Origin, Characteristic 2 – Quality Grade, etc.).
  • Segmentation Strategy
    • You can define multiple segmentation strategies for a segmentation structure. In a segmentation strategy  segmentation strategy you can define the rules to map requirement and stock segment values and much more. This has following sub nodes:
      • ARUN/MRP – Map requirement segments to stock segments (To be discussed in a separate blog later)
      • ATP Pool – Define Pool segment values for ATP (To be discussed in a separate blog later)
      • Dependent Requirements – Used when to determine requirement segments of components based on stock segment of main item (e.g. Planned Order components or Production Order components)
      • Requirement to Stock conversion – Used to determine stock segment of MRP stock proposals
      • Planned Independent Requirements – Used to consume PIR requirement segments for desired SO/STOs requirement segments
    • Segmentation Scope: Define if the scope of segmentation is relevant for both demand and supply (Scope 1) or only for demand (Scope 2):
      • Scope 1 Segmentation (Both Requirement and Stock Segments): A typical use of physical segmentation such as Country of Origin, Product Revision, Quality Grades are relevant for both demand and supply segmentation, and the strategy should be set as Scope 1
      • Scope 2 Segmentation (Only Stock Segment): Certain logical segmentation uses cases such as channel based (ECOM, Retail and Wholesale) relevant only from demand side and hence the segmentation strategy is recommended to be set as Scope 2. (Note – Logical Segmentation set as Scope 1 will have downstream impacts in deliveries and warehouse operations and should be evaluated case to case basis)

Below steps demonstrate setting up of basic segmentation strategy and assigning to material master:

Step 1 – Create Segmentation Characteristics

  1. Go to transaction CT04
  2. Enter below details and save the characteristics

 Step 2 – Set up Segmentation Structure

  1. Go to transaction ‘SGT_SETUP’ and click on ‘Create’. Enter the structure name and description and save.

  1. Once saved, go to change mode and double click on the structure. Enter the characteristics created in step 1. Once done click save.

Note – If you want to enable blank values for any of the characteristics, ensure that you select ‘Enable blank’ against each characteristic in above grid screen.

Step 3 – Create Segmentation Strategy

  1. Go to change mode of the segmentation strategy and then right click on structure created in step 2 and select ‘Add Segmentation Strategy’ and click on save.

  1. Go to Change mode and then double click on the segmentation strategy. You can manually add the segmentation values or generate them as shown below

  1. Generate Segmentation strategy once above steps are completed.

In above step, system generates 1:1 Segmentation for all notes by default. We will discuss the details of individual nodes later.

Step 4 – Assign Segmentation Strategy to Material Master

  1. Once Segmentation setup is completed, assign the segmentation strategy to the material as shown below.
  2. ‘Assign the segmentation strategy’ at Material level in ‘Basic Data 2’ Tab

 

  1. ‘Assign the segmentation strategy’ at Plant level in ‘Plant data/stor.1’ Tab and save the material.

Key points to be noted:

  1. You observe that batch management will be automatically set when segmentation is activated
  2. You can have segmentation relevancy for one plant and not for another plant for the same material
  3. You can have different segmentation strategies for different plants for the same material if they belong to same segmentation structure

Hope this blog helps to provide a basic understanding of “Segmentation” to define appropriate settings to setup and assign to a material.

SAP Help Documentation:

  1. Segmentation

Blog Series:

  1. Demand and Supply Segmentation in S/4 HANA - Segmentation Setup

entry.title: How Do I Activate a Situation Type in the Extended Framework?
entry.content:
Situation Handling notifies users about issues requiring their attention. You may choose SAP delivered situation templates or design your own use cases. For the extended framework for Situation Handling, we often discussed how you can create custom situation templates. The framework comes also with SAP delivered use cases that you can easily activate.

This blog post explains how you can activate situation types from SAP delivered situation templates with the extended framework for Situation Handling. It has been available since SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2202 and SAP S/4HANA 2021 FPS02.

 

SAP Delivered Use Cases

Looking for Situation Handling use cases? Visit SAP Help and navigate to Situation Handling, then choose Use Cases. You can narrow your results by filtering for Situation Template Type (either extended object-based or message-based), area, and topic. To learn more about a specific use case, simply check the link in the Situation Template column for a detailed description.

Use Case

For illustration purposes we use the situation template Expiration of Purchase Contracts (PROC_ABOUT_TO_EXPIRE_CTR). Purchasers get a heads-up for purchase contracts that are about to expire.

 

Activation Overview for 2SL and 3SL

Before discussing the activation steps in detail, let me point out the differences between system landscapes and their specific transport paths.

In the extended framework for Situation Handling, you create a situation type from a situation template. Situation templates are only visible in the quality system of a 2-system landscape (2SL) and the development system of a  3-system-landscape (3SL). The situation type needs to be transported to the production system. The transport paths differ between 2SL and 3SL.

Transport the Situation Type in 2SL

In a 2-system-landscape, you transport the situation type from the quality system to the production system.

  • In the quality system you
    • Create a situation type from a situation template
    • Test if the configuration alerts the right users about the situation
    • Export the well-defined situation type
  • In the production system you
    • Import the situation type
    • Since the data usually differs between quality and production system you need to enter the appropriate values for the condition filters
    • Enable the situation type

Transports in 2-System-Landscape

Transport the Situation Type in 3SL

In a 3-system-landscape, transports are required from the development system to both the test and production system. In a 3SL environment you may also have different tenants for development, customizing, and parallel customizing that need to be considered as well.

  • On the customizing tenant in the development system you
    • Create a situation type from a situation template
    • Export the situation type
  • On the test tenant in the test system you
    • Import the situation type
    • Since the data usually differs between systems you need to enter the appropriate values for the condition filters
    • Test if the configuration alerts the right users about the situation
    • Forward the well-defined situation type
  • On the production tenant in the production system you
    • Import the forwarded situation type
    • Enter the appropriate values for the condition filters
    • Enable the situation type

Transports in 3-System-Landscape

Detailed Activation of a Situation Type in 3SL

Now let’s check the detailed steps for a sample use case in a 3-system-landscape as it is more comprehensive. In 2SL the export / import mechanism resembles the one from development system to test system in 3SL.

Depending on your system setting, the transports may also be processed automatically. For optimal clarity, the blog post discusses the transport steps through manual triggers.

Development System

Create a Situation Type from a Situation Template

Log-in as business process configuration expert on the customizing tenant in the development system. Open the Manage Situation Types – Extended app where you see a list of SAP delivered templates. Use the search option to find the template for our use case Expiration of Purchase Contracts (PROC_ABOUT_TO_EXPIRE_CTR). Select the template and an object page opens. In the header, select Create Situation Type.

Create situation type from situation template

A new page opens with the situation type that contains all information from the situation template. You just need to enter an ID, Name and Description. The type is configured and ready to use. Depending on your requirements you could adapt the settings, for example, by adding filter values for the conditions, changing texts or rescheduling the batch job.

I recommend monitoring the instances to see how situations are handled.

Monitor situations

Let’s check the relevant settings and understand how the configuration works.

There are two Situation Triggers that detect situations and cover different usage scenarios. While the batch-based trigger checks for situations in the background, the event-based trigger reacts on every active change of the contract.

Situation triggers

The situation triggers cover the following cases:

  • The Event-Based Trigger checks the conditions whenever you create or update a purchase contract.
    • Condition 1 checks if the validity period of a purchase contract expires within the next 30 days and triggers a situation. The information displayed to the user is defined in Situation Display number 1.
    • Condition 2 is only executed if condition 1 did not match. That is, the validity period is not within the next 30 days, for instance when the contract got extended, and the situation is closed. As nobody will be informed about the closure, the texts are empty (Situation Display number 2).

Event-based trigger

  • In addition to the events, the Batch-Based Trigger regularly checks the validity end date according to the batch schedule.
    • Condition 1 checks if the validity period of a purchase contract expires within the next 30 days and triggers a situation.

Batch-based trigger

The configuration for the Batch Job Scheduling specifies that the contract dates are verified every 3 hours throughout the day, beginning at 6:00 a.m.

Batch job scheduling

Export the Situation Type

Log-in as administrator on the customizing tenant in the development system. Open the Export Software Collection app.

  • On top of the table choose Create Software Collection and a new page opens
  • Choose Add Items and select the situation type you created
  • On the header select Check and wait until the check is processed successfully
  • Then choose Export and wait until the export is finished successfully

Test System

Import the Situation Type

Log-in as administrator on the test tenant in the test system. Open the Import Collection app.

  • Choose your software collection and a new page opens
  • On the header select Manual Import and wait until the check is processed successfully
  • Then choose Import on the bottom and wait until the import is finished successfully

Test the Situation Type

As business process specialist you can test the situation type on the corresponding tenant.

Make sure that the situation type is enabled.

Activate the situation type

Then maintain the conditions. The filter values are not transported since they may differ in the various systems and tenants.

  • Select the Event-Based Trigger and a new page opens
    • Condition 1 select Edit and enter the filter values
      • Ext. Reference ID: enter Empty, using the value help
      • Validity Period End: enter Next30Days, using the value help
    • Condition 2 select Edit and enter the filter values
      • Ext. Reference ID: enter Empty, using the value help
    • Go back to the main page

Event-based trigger

  • Select the Batch-Based Trigger and a new page opens
    • Condition 1 select Edit and enter the filter values
      • Ext. Reference ID: enter Empty, using the value help
      • Validity Period End: enter Next30Days, using the value help
    • Go back to the main page

Batch-based trigger

With the situation triggers, you defined when a situation is detected. Now, you need to define who should be informed about situations. Maintain the team(s) in the Manage Teams and Responsibilities app and ensure that at least one team is enabled. The filter settings work the same way as with the standard framework for Situation Handling.

Situation recipients

Now you can test the situation type in your business area with the corresponding application data. You can check if there are purchase contracts that match the conditions by choosing Trigger Batch and Manage Instances.

Test the type with trigger batch

If no situations were detected you can log-in as purchaser and create or manipulate a purchasing contract. Add an end date within the next 30 days and the save event will trigger a situation.

When you log-in as a team member of the recipients team you can also check, if you were alerted about the situation.

After the successful test you push the situation type to the production system.

Forward the Situation Type

Log-in as administrator and open the Import Collection app.

  • Select your software collection and a new page opens
  • On the header select Forward and wait until the validation is processed successfully
  • Then choose Forward on the bottom and wait until the export is finished successfully

Production System

Import Collection to Production System

As administrator open the Import Collection app on the production tenant in the production system.

  • Choose your software collection and a new page opens
  • On the header select Manual Import and wait until the check is processed successfully
  • Then choose Import on the bottom and wait until the import is finished successfully

Activate the Situation Type

Log-in as business process specialist and open the Manage Situation Types – Extended app. Like in the test system, you need to maintain the situation type.

  • Enable the situation type
  • Add the filter values for the trigger conditions
  • Ensure that at least one recipient team is enabled

Now you are all set.

Further Information

You find many more blog posts in the Best Practices section of Intelligent Situation Handling in the SAP Community. And you can visit SAP Help Portal for further guidance on Situation Handling.

entry.title: Introducing SAP Cloud ERP Private, Nonproduction Usage: A Flexible Path to SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud
entry.content:
Introduction

Many SAP ERP Central Component (SAP ECC) cloud customers face a necessary upgrade because of upcoming end-of-support deadlines for SAP ERP 6.0 private cloud systems on Enhancement Packages 6-8 (mainstream maintenance ending in 2027 and extended maintenance in 2030).

SAP ECC (SAP ECC 6.0 with Enhancement Package 6 to 8 ) will move to Customer Specific Maintenance starting 1 January 2031. This also means that the SAP ERP, private edition offering will effectively have an end of availability on 31 December 2030. Because of this, many customers will face a time-sensitive transition period. During this time, they may need to run both their existing SAP ERP 6.0 system and the new SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud system in parallel—especially for non-production environments like testing, development, and QA during migration.

Having both environments available can accelerate transformation initiatives, allowing teams to leverage bundled cloud components for innovation, testing, process redesign, and early adoption of new capabilities while the legacy system continues to support ongoing operations.

At the same time, customers seeking a more cost-optimized transition path now have a new option that provides earlier access to a non-production environment without requiring a full parallel subscription. This gives customers greater flexibility to plan and execute their transformation while managing overall subscription costs.

To support this approach, we are introducing SAP Cloud ERP Private, nonproduction usage. This offering enables unlimited non-production use of SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud during the transition period, allowing customers to perform migration and testing activities without incurring the cost of a full production subscription.

How this helps, for example.

Explanation:

  • Without SAP Cloud ERP Private, nonproduction usage 
    In Year 2, the SAP ERP, private edition subscription is needed for the production systems, but as non-productive environments are gradually upgraded to the new S/4HANA Private Cloud version, they also need SAP Cloud ERP Private subscription. This overlap leads to simultaneous subscription costs of both SAP ERP, private edition and new SAP Cloud ERP Private systems. 
  • With SAP Cloud ERP Private, nonproduction usage 
    In Year 2, production continues under the old SAP ERP, private edition subscription, while the non-productive environments are covered by the new SAP Cloud ERP Private, nonproduction usage product. SAP Cloud ERP Private, nonproduction usage can be applied to an unlimited number of non-productive systems, allowing full coverage without triggering a full double subscription. In Year 3, all environments have migrated and can now be covered with the normal SAP Cloud ERP Private subscription.

Benefits of SAP Cloud ERP Private, nonproduction usage  

  • Removes simultaneous subscriptions during the transition phase 
  • Supports testing and validation without the need for exact timing of the switch to a new subscription 
  • Access to the SAP AI portfolio in the nonproduction environment, not available with SAP ECC [SAP ERP 6.0]
  • You only pay for limited support for the non-productive systems during the migration period
  • Covers an unlimited number of non-productive systems 
  • Gives customers the flexibility to plan and execute the migration properly
  • The transition period can be as long as needed, keeping the end of support date in mind.
  • SAP Cloud ERP Private, nonproduction usage is an addition to the SAP ERP, private edition subscription, extending the product support to S/4HANA Private Cloud in the transition phase. 
  • Helps organizations meet upgrade deadlines without budget overruns

entry.title: NEW! - Enterprise management layer for SAP S/4HANA - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 FSP00
entry.content:
On Monday March 2nd, 2026, the enterprise management layer team has just released its latest template system based on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 FSP00.

The enterprise management layer is not to be confused with model company for SAP Business Suite.

  • The focus of the enterprise management layer template is large multinational organizations operating in many countries around the world. It provides the foundation of your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition by covering all Line of Business. It is by design industry agnostic, so that it can easily be used in all the industries covered by SAP S/4HANA.
  • Model company for SAP Business Suite focuses on the industry-specific content and is available for Germany and the United States.

Some of the Model company for SAP Business Suite is compatible and can be applied on top of the enterprise management layer.

The new release of the enterprise management layer is based on a selected number of scope items (220+) from SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and specific content only available in the enterprise management layer. It covers up to 49 countries; 43 available through SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and 6 only available through the content built by the enterprise management layer team. The system is delivered with 25 languages and is fully Fiori enabled.

The offering is split under in three groups as depicted below:

  • Standard Scope: activation of SAP Best Practices scope items that do not require a specific SAP license other than the core SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Scope Options: activation of SAP Best Practices scope items that require specific SAP license
  • Service Options: activation of content only available in the enterprise management layer

Compared to the previous versions of the enterprise management layer, besides the fact that the content deployed by the activation of SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition is based on the updated 2025 FPS00 content, the following specifics shall be mentioned.

New scope items:

  • Treasury and Risk Management (Optional scope):
    • TRM12 - Master Data: Treasury Management*
  • Order and Contract Management (Standard scope)
    • 3TD - Internal Commissions Settlement for Sales (replaces OTC30)
  • Service Operations & Processes (Standard scope)
    • 7GB - In-House Service (mainly description change)
  • Extended Warehouse Management (Embedded) (Optional scope)
    • 7VC - Synchronous Goods Receipts in EWM - External Procurement
    • 7VD - Material Replenishment with Kanban – Stock Transfer within EWM 
    • 7GP - Basic TM/EWM – Inbound Advanced Shipping and Receiving 
    • 7GQ - Basic TM/EWM – Outbound Advanced Shipping and Receiving
    • WM05D - Cross-Plant Stock Transfer with EWM in both Plants with Different Serial Number Profiles*
  • Advanced Shipping & Receiving (Optional scope)
    • 7VE - Advanced TM-EWM – Inbound Advanced Shipping and Receiving
    • 7VF - Advanced TM-EWM - Outbound Advanced Shipping and Receiving
  • Advanced Variant Configuration (Optional scope)
    • 7KW - Customer Returns for Material with Variant Configuration
  • Product Compliance (Optional scope)
    • 7DO Product Compliance Document Authoring
  • Embedded Production Planning and Detailed Scheduling (ePP/DS) (Optional scope)
    • 7UV Make – to – Stock Production - Discrete Manufacturing
    • 7V9 Make – to - Order Production  -  Discrete Manufacturing

* enterprise management specific

Removed scope items:

  • Order and Contract Management
    • 2QS - Analytics for Sales - Central Stock with Returns
    • OTC30 - Internal Commissions Settlement for Sales (replaced by 3TD)* 
  • Extended Warehouse Management (Embedded) 
    • WM01 - Synchronous Goods Receipts in EWM  For External Procurement (replaced by 7VC)*
    • WM02 - Synchronous Goods Receipts in EWM for Kanban (replaced by 7VD)*

* enterprise management specific

Updated Localization content:

CountryFeatureDescription
ATAnnual Financial StatementOBSOLETE: Use the Annual Financial Statement App to prepare an XML file that contains the annual financial statements of your company that you submit to the Austrian commercial register. You submit the data using the ERV-JAb data service (version 03.31).
ATAnnual Financial StatementNew report supporting ERV JAb 4.0 is available.
BEAnnual Sales ListingUPDATED: The Report has been adjusted so that the Representative information is optional and only populated in the XML, if the related Reporting Parameters are maintained.
BEPayment formatsThe following Payment Formats are available BE_CGI_XML_CT_V9 and BE_CGI_XML_DD_V8.
CATax Jurisdiction Codes in Document and Reporting ComplianceIt is possible to select more than one Tax Jurisdiction Code as part of the selection criteria of reports Canada QST Return, Canada PST British Columbia Return, Canada PST Saskatchewan Return, and Canada PST Manitoba Return.
CHProcessing Incoming Payments with LSV+ ReferenceReferred to the user-exit and solution required to populate the LSV+ Reference if required and how to use it in incoming payment processing.
CHCash Flow StatementYou can generate a Cash Flow Statement report using the Indirect Method (also known as C. Tableau des flux de trésorerie).
CLeBoleta additional fieldsThe following fields are populated in the XML:
• CorreoRecep - refers to the re-ceiver e-mail address
• TelefonoRecep - refers to the receiver telephone number
• MedioPago - refers to the pay-ment method
CNCash Flow StatementsOBSOLETE: Companies in China are required to generate a statement about the changes in their cash and cash equivalents in each period. You can prepare the cash flow statement in the required format as defined by law
CNCash Flow StatementNew features are available such as data preview, correction phase and manual adjustments.
CNBalance Sheet / Income StatementNew features are available such as being able to call the Display G/L Account Balances - Summary/Journal Entry Views app from within.
CNSplitting Cells with Multiple ValuesWith this feature, you can split cells with multiple values when exporting to a spreadsheet by choosing the option Split cells with multiple values.
CNAging Report (Accounts Receivable/Accounts Payable)UPDATED: A new selection criterion Reporting Currency is available and allows the user to select between Local Currency, Functional Currency, Global Currency, and First Freely-Defined Currency to display the amounts.
COUGPP ReportsThe UGPP reports allow you to generate your Trial Balance (report category CO_UGPP_TRIAL_BALANCE) and Auxiliary of Accounts (report category CO_UGPP_AUX_ACCT) and fulfill the statutory reporting requirements from the Unidad de Gestión Pensional y Parafiscales. The output is in a CSV format.
COAccount Balance Ledger ReportingThe Monthly Ledger (report category CO_ACCT_BAL_LEDGER) report enables you to generate a monthly balance of accounts and subaccounts. The output is in a CSV format.
CODIAN 350 Withholding Tax FormYou can generate the DIAN 350 Withholding Tax Report which reports on all types of Withholding Tax and generates the DIAN 350 PDF Form.
DEE-BilanzUPDATED: The E-Bilanz (electronic financial statement for Germany) is the required electronic tax filing in Germany. Updated with latest Taxonomy.
DEReduced Rate on User RightsWhen processing foreign supplier invoices related to User Rights a lower withholding tax rate should be applied for transactions amounting to up to EUR 10,000 in case there is a Double Tax Agreement between Germany and the country of the supplier
DEVAT Return FormatUPDATED: A new XSD format starting January 1st, 2026 is available.
ESEC Sales List Modelo 349 – Credit Memo ReferencingAll relevant credit memo must reference an original invoice. To reference credit memo, the Rebate Reprocessing Activity can be used.
ESCadastral ReferenceModelo 115 reports on withholding tax related to income from property renting or leasing. 
ESTax Register Book to SII through SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud EditionUPDATED: it is now possible to exchange the electronic documents using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition
ESB2G invoices through SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud EditionUPDATED: it is now possible to exchange the B2G electronic invoices using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition
ESVERI*FACTUYou can exchange electronic invoices through the VERI*FACTU system
FRPayment FormatsNew Payment Format FR_CGI_XML_CT_V9 available
GBPayment FormatsNew Payment Formats GB_CGI_XML_CT_V9 and GB_CGI_XML_DD_V8 are available
HRFiscalizationFiscalization refers to a set of measures implemented by companies in Croatia that provide an overview of cash transaction revenues. When a buyer pays the seller by cash, the probability of the seller evading tax by not declaring the cash payments is very high. To prevent this, the tax authority in Croatia, has instructed sellers to declare all their cash transactions.
HROverdue Receivable (OPZ-STAT-1)The Overdue Receivable report can be executed through Document and Reporting Compliance
HRWithholding Tax ReportingYou can report on suppliers’ transactions subject to Withholding Tax such as:
• Dividends and shares
• Services of foreign legal entities such as business consultancy, audit, tax consultancy, research markets etc.
• Interests other than the pre-scribed exemptions in accord-ance with the provisions of arti-cle 31 of the Corporate Income Tax Act
• Payment of copyright and other intellectual property rights
• Service of companies in other countries
HRB2B eInvoicingFrom January 1st, 2026, all B2B invoices must be exchanged electronically. The system now generates electronic documents for B2B transactions.
You can also generate electronic docu-ments for customer payments.
HUMonthly VAT Declaration Format (2025)UPDATED: New chapter about VAT Return Form 2665A version 2, and the latest requirements in terms of Reverse Charge Tax for some specific products
IDBPPU Withholding Tax Report The BPPU Withholding Tax Report is known locally as the Bukti Potong Pajak Unifikasi and generates an XML file with the related Withholding Tax levies.
IDBPNR Withholding Tax Report for Foreign VendorsThe BPNR Withholding Tax Report is known locally as the Bukti Potong Non-Residen and generates an XML file with the related Withholding Tax levies.
IDVAT OUT (CORETAX)The VAT OUT (Coretax) report enables you to generate a list of VAT items on sales to customers in an XML file. You can download the generated XML file and upload it to the Coretax system.
KREntertainment Expenses ReportProviding additional information about the prerequisites to run the entertainment expense report and the report output.
KRVAT ReportProviding additional information about the prerequisites for generating a successful VAT Report and the various mandatory/optional outputs supported.
KRMonthly Submission of Withholding Tax ReportsIt is now possible to submit the Withholding Tax reports for Business and Other Income monthly.
LUSAF-TArticle 70 of the Luxembourg VAT law and Administrative Circular nº 742, published in the Mémorial A-206 (Official Journal of the Grand Duchy of Luxembourg), effectively amending the VAT Law requirements determines that all businesses using electronic accounting systems are required to provide their bookkeeping data in a legible and intelligible electronic format, upon request by the Luxembourg Tax Authorities, in the form of the FAIA file (Fichier d'Audit Informatisé AED).
UPDATED: Generation of Periodic VAT Return through Document and Reporting Compliance
LUNew Payment FormatsThe following Payment Formats are available LU_CGI_XML_CT_V9 and LU_CGI_XML_DD_V8.
LUFixed Assets Appendix of Annual VAT ReturnThis report enables you to generate an XML file with fixed assets information to be submitted together with the Annual VAT Return report to the tax authorities through the eCDF platform.
MXWithholding Tax DeclarationOBSOLETE: This report is now obsolete and replaced by the electronic submission of Withholding Tax Certificates (see OSS note 3361404 - Sunset of Mexico Statutory Report for Withholding Tax reporting in DRC)
NLFinancial Audit FileUPDATED: The Financial Audit File is a standard for creating a xml file containing relevant data in Financial Accounting for the Netherlands. The file contains master data, opening balances and transactions that were processed during a certain period. The Financial Audit File is used by the Dutch tax authorities and approved accountants to perform audits of the financial administration of Dutch companies.
The file version available is 4.0
NOGeneral Ledger Account Line Items, Customer Line Items and Supplier Line Items ReportsA new configuration parameter has been introduced to limit the number of line items for the PDF generation to avoid the issues in ADS Server
PLReverse Charge VAT Declaration (Form VAT-27 (2))OBSOLETE: In Poland, certain transactions are subject to reverse charging. This means that the obligation to settle the applicable VAT amount rests with the purchaser and not the ven-dor. The vendor issues an invoice for 0% VAT (in enterprise management layer Tax Codes A6 and A7 are available) and then reports the reverse charge to the tax authorities through a supplement to the standard periodic VAT declaration.
This supplement is the Reverse Charge VAT Declaration (form 27) provided to the authorities in XML format.
PLOfficial Document NumberYou are required by law to number the documents you post to your customers, for example, customer invoices or credit memos, following defined rules defined by the tax authorities. Each invoice successfully submitted to the KSeF (National System of e-Invoices) receives a unique identifier known as the KSeF Invoice ID, which is distinct from the invoice ID (number) issued by the sender.
PLSAF-T JPK_WBThe SAF-T Bank Statements report (PL_SAFT_WB) enables you to generate the report on bank account transactions in an XML file format. 
PLWithholding TaxAdded a chapter about Withholding Tax requirements and set up in enterprise management layer.
PLAlgorithm for KSeF Reference Numbers You can use algorithm 805 in your Posting Rules to clear open items based on the KSeF Reference Number
PTPurchase Account ProcedureUPDATED: If Purchase Account Management is active, which is the case for Portugal, additional postings are carried out during the posting of receipts of goods and invoices.
PTAsset ClassesUPDATED: Specific Asset Classes relevant to Portugal are available in enterprise management layer. 
PTQR Code UPDATED: A QR code and additional information such as the ACTUD number are printed on the billing documents generated from Sales and Distribution
PTAdditional Tax Box Structure Types for the Periodic VAT Return and Annexes ReportIf your main location is not the continent, but the Azores or Madeira, you can use new parameters to set your Tax Box Structures accordingly
ROAccount CardReport FIŞĂ DE CONT can now be reported through Document Reporting and Compliance
RODocument JournalReport REGISTRUL JURNAL can now be reported through Document Reporting and Compliance
ROCash JournalThe Romania Cash Journal report enables you to generate your Cash Journal (local name: REGISTRUL DE CASĂ).
ROGeneral Ledger with Corresponding AccountsReport CARTEA MARE can now be reported through Document Reporting and Compliance
SAVendor CertificatesUPDATED: Suppliers are required to maintain several certificates that are pre-requisites for doing business in the country. For example, all suppli-ers that have 100,000 Saudi Riyals in capital are legally required to register with the Commercial Register Office and are issued a commercial registration certificate with a Commercial Registration Number (CRN).
These certificates are only valid for a limited time and must be re-newed periodically.
Added new Business Partner Certificates functionality.
SAVAT Return FormatUPDATED: The Saudi Arabia VAT Return statutory report has been updated to generate a PDF output that is like the online form provided by GAZT.
If you have maintained the International Address Version in Arabic in your Company Code the address is populated in Arabic in the Arabic Output Form
SKVAT ReturnA new VAT Return form is mandatory from July 1st, 2025, following the introduction of the new reverse charge mechanism on imports from outside the EU and new VAT rates.
THBranch Code optional for non-VAT registered Business PartnersThe Branch Code is now optional for non-VAT registered Business Partners (foreign or natural person)
TRNew Value Mapping UBL_DOC_TYPEA new value mapping UBL_DOC_TYPE is available for Delivery Notes
XXDisplay G/L Account Balances in Country/Region Specific FormatUPDATED: This App displays general ledger account balances for specified fiscal periods and in a format based on country/region -specific accounting practices.
From enterprise management layer based on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025, the App has been renamed.
XXLocal Chart of AccountsUPDATED: List of Local Chart of Accounts available in enterprise management layer and which Financial Statement Versions is using them
XXFinancial Statement VersionsUPDATED: List of Financial Statement Versions available in enterprise management layer.
XXFlexible Billing Document NumberingUPDATED: Reference to Flexible Billing Document Numbering on how to address country specific billing document numbering required in Spain and Portugal
XXOutgoing Payment MethodsUPDATED: list of outgoing payment methods per country including payment format
XXIncoming Payment MethodsUPDATED: list of incoming payment methods per country including payment format
XXSpecial features in Document Reporting ComplianceAdded a note about the need to activate Business Function GLO_DRC to activate the special features or not.
XXInternational Address VersionsReferring to the functionality of International Address Versions and how to activate them
XXCountry-specific fields in Manage Journal EntriesReferring to which Country specific fields are available in App Manage Journal Entries 
XXRun Statutory Reports App UpdatesVarious new features are available in the Run Statutory Reports App
XXManage Document and Reporting Compliance AppUPDATED: Update list of Fiori Apps and relevant SAP Business Roles

entry.title: What’s New in Asset Management in SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 FPS01
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 FPS01 release delivers a strong collection of enhancements across Asset Management, with improvements in maintenance execution, safety, planning, technical object management, and extensibility.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (the foundation of SAP Cloud ERP Private) strengthens efficient, data‑driven maintenance in this release while elevating user experience and process consistency across the entire asset lifecycle.

In this blog post, you’ll find a curated overview of the most important innovations, grouped by theme and written for maintenance technicians, planners, reliability engineers, and integration partners who rely on SAP to keep operations running safely and efficiently.

For a broader look at what’s new across other lines of business in the SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 FPS01 release, don’t miss the blog by Yannick Peterschmitt: What’s New in the SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 FPS1

Table of content:

  • Safety, compliance & field execution excellence
    - Isolation Management in Permit to Work – Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) 
    - Associated work permits in Manage Maintenance Orders app 
    - Inspection checklist enhancements 
    - After‑job recording for unplanned work 
    - New capabilities in Perform Maintenance Jobs app
  • Maintenance request & work pack enhancements
    - Improvements in Screen Maintenance Requests app 
    - Custom logic to add e‑mail recipients for job pack outputs
  • Planning and strategy enhancements
    - Additional functionality in Manage Maintenance Plans and Items apps 
    - Extended planning bucket capabilities 
    - Improvements for phase control code and analytics based on phases
  • Master data and technical object innovations
    - Reuse value help app for technical objects 
    - Hierarchy structure view for reference structures
  •  Resource Scheduling enhancements (43R)
    - Replanning for operations in process 
    - Configuration for utilization calculation 
    - New BAdIs for Scheduling Logic (Manage Schedules – F3326)
  • SAP Asset Performance Management 
  • More information

Safety, compliance & field execution excellence

Isolation Management in Permit to Work – Lockout/Tagout (LOTO)

The 2025 FPS01 release introduces a fully embedded Isolation Management capability that brings the complete Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) lifecycle into SAP S/4HANA Asset Management. Through new configuration options and dedicated Fiori apps, maintenance organizations can structure and govern how isolation activities are defined, prepared, approved, and executed.

A new customizing section enables planning plants to specify isolation certificate behavior, certificate and template types, number ranges, and approval setups. The Manage Isolation Certificate Templates app (F8505) allows users to build reusable templates by adding technical objects, attaching work permit templates, assigning approvers, and adding supporting documents, ensuring consistent and compliant LOTO preparation.

Manage Isolation Certificate Templates app (F8505)

 

With the Manage Isolation Certificates app (F8504), users can create certificates from scratch or from a predefined template, add and process isolation items, move them through operational cycles such as tagging, testing, and untagging, assign work permits and approvals, and attach relevant documents. Printable outputs for certificates, lists, and tags support on‑site execution.

These innovations strengthen safety compliance and reduce operational risk through modernized digital isolation management, improves coordination between operations and maintenance to boost asset productivity, enhances the handling of technical objects during LOTO procedures, and increases overall usability of the permit‑to‑work process.

Check this Road Map item: Isolation management in permit to work (lockout tagout)

Associated work permits in Manage Maintenance Orders app

Work permits are now seamlessly integrated into the Manage Maintenance Orders (F4604) app, becoming available whenever a Permit‑to‑Work profile is maintained for the planning plant. A dedicated Work Permits section provides immediate visibility of permit details, including approval status, implemented precautions, certificate progress, and handout information.

Users can navigate directly to the related permit or create a new one linked to the order and planning plant. In addition, a Fiori‑based mass creation job enables work permits to be generated for multiple orders at once, using filtering criteria and template determination to streamline preparation and ensure consistent alignment with Work Clearance Management.

Read more about it: Mass Generation of Permit to Work Objects | SAP Help Portal

Work permits section in Manage Maintenance Orders (F4604) app

 

Inspection checklist enhancements

Inspection checklist processing receives several refinements that improve configuration, transparency, and usability across maintenance workflows. A new customizing knot centralizes all checklist‑related settings, creating a clearer foundation for managing current and future checklist configurations. Updated output forms now reflect material‑based checklists, covering serial‑number and component information in maintenance orders and work packs, ensuring documents accurately represent checklist content.

Enhancements in the Manage Inspection Checklists app (F6607) introduce richer detail in inspection lots, including quantities, reservation data, component references, serial numbers (if enabled), and new filtering options such as technical object and inspection lot status. Task lists now display a material checklist indicator, and the related API has been expanded to support reading, creating, and updating this field.

Within Manage Maintenance Orders app (F5241), checklist types are visible at operation level, cross‑app navigation to inspection checklists is supported, and actions such as “Generate Checklist” streamline preparation. Authorization checks for checklist types help ensure only permitted users can assign or modify certain checklist categories, providing stronger control over sensitive checklist usage.

Find more in the Road Map: SAP Road Map Explorer

Manage Inspection Checklists and Manage Maintenance Orders apps

After‑job recording for unplanned work

Technicians can now document maintenance activities that were performed immediately during an ad‑hoc repair, even when no prior order or confirmation existed. Using the new Confirmation of Unplanned Work processing context, they record failure data, measurements, consumed components, attachments, and time confirmations after completing the physical repair, ensuring proper traceability without delaying the actual fix.

A dedicated customizing knot controls default behaviors such as notification type, priority, and order type, and determines whether statuses like Work Completed or Job Finished are automatically applied. Authorization checks ensure only permitted users can create or submit unplanned work confirmations. Technicians can start the process either from the Perform Maintenance Jobs (F5104A) app, where the context is preselected, or from Create Maintenance Request (F1511A), where it must be chosen manually.

Once submitted, the system creates the necessary order operation (often directly in the MAIN execution stage) and enables technicians to capture all required details in a structured flow. Additional behavior, such as phase transitions or automatic technical completion, follows plant‑specific customizing settings.

Check more in the Road Map: SAP Road Map Explorer

Confirmation of Unplanned Work

 

 

New capabilities in Perform Maintenance Jobs app

The Perform Maintenance Jobs app (F5104A) receives numerous usability and functional enhancements that make job execution, time recording, and material handling more transparent and efficient. Key improvements include:

Configuration & time recording

  • New customizing knot for clearer setup of all F5104A‑related configuration steps.
  • Flexible defaulting of employee for time confirmations (logged‑in user, main technician, previous confirmation, or empty).
  • Introduction of Degree of Progress to capture physical work completion independently from actual work time, with UI slider and defaults based on last confirmation.

Perform Maintenance Jobs app (F5104A)

Job pausing & transparency

  • New pause codes for documenting why a job is paused, with optional or mandatory entry modes and full history tracking.

Notification processing

  • Long text templates can be applied during malfunction data capture, supporting guided, structured input and respecting notification type text rules.

Technical object visibility

  • New overview of object list entries with processing‑indicator editing (if authorized) and cross‑app navigation.

Material handling improvements

  • Transparency for EWM‑managed materials, automatically disabling Post/Return actions when required.
  • Better handling for batch‑managed materials, including mandatory batch entry and smart‑link navigation from material numbers.
  • Enhanced support for serialized materials, including value help and duplicate‑number checks during posting.

List page enhancements

  • A redesigned list table with more visible results, improved column management, context menu, and richer notification quick info.

Redesigned list table in Perform Maintenance Jobs app

Maintenance request & work pack enhancements

Improvements in Screen Maintenance Requests app

The Screen Maintenance Requests (F4072) Fiori app now adopts a flexible column layout so users can view the request list alongside its details in resizable, side‑by‑side columns, reducing back‑and‑forth navigation and accelerating review.

In addition, a new Change Scheduling action enables mass updates of open notifications for key scheduling fields (Priority, Maintenance Event, Required Start, Required Finish, and Final Due Date) directly from the list page; changes are captured in the History tab for traceability of updates to consequence and likelihood values.

Screen Maintenance Requests (F4072) app

 

Custom logic to add e‑mail recipients for job pack outputs

A new enhancement makes it easier to distribute job pack and work pack outputs to all relevant stakeholders. Using a dedicated BAdI, planners can now add additional e‑mail recipients to work pack output items, such as pick lists or instructions, through Output Parameter Determination. This logic can be activated for specific output types and e‑mail channels (To, Cc, Bcc), and supports flexible recipient management via custom tables, reusable elements, or SAP distribution lists.

At the same time, work pack creation becomes more versatile. The previous limitation restricting creation to execution or post‑execution phases is removed; planners can now enable work pack creation across the entire maintenance order lifecycle based on work pack type settings. New customizing options allow organizations to manage where restrictions apply for both phase and non‑phase‑enabled orders, increasing control and flexibility.

Custom logic to add e‑mail recipients for job pack outputs

Planning and strategy enhancements

Additional functionality in Manage Maintenance Plans and Items apps

Maintenance planners gain more flexibility and control through several usability improvements in the Manage Maintenance Plan (F5325) and Manage Maintenance Item (F5356) apps. The plan app now allows users to search maintenance plans directly by description, making it easier to identify and retrieve relevant plans.

In the maintenance item app, planners can filter items by maintenance plan status and display the plan status directly in the item list, offering clearer visibility into which items belong to which plan stage.

Manage Maintenance Plan (F5325) and Manage Maintenance Item (F5356) apps

Extended planning bucket capabilities

The planning bucket framework is expanded to give maintenance planners more control over how backlogs are grouped and prepared for execution. With this enhancement, users can define a broader and more flexible range of maintenance backlogs to be included in a reference or event-based planning bucket. This includes the ability to add additional grouping fields, enabling maintenance teams to organize work in ways that reflect real operational structures or business priorities.

For example, planners can now link an improvement project to a planning bucket, allowing all backlogs associated with that project to be bundled into a single, coherent scope. This creates a more holistic view of the work that needs to be scheduled and makes it easier to consolidate activities that naturally belong together.

These enhancements optimize the scope of maintenance backlogs planned within a given period, while giving planners the flexibility to reprioritize and adjust the bucket contents based on available capacity, budget, and strategic initiatives.

Improvements for phase control code and analytics based on phases

This enhancement strengthens the way maintenance organizations use the phase‑based maintenance model by improving both the usability of phase control codes and the analytics available around phase progression.

The definition of phase control codes has been simplified, so the same codes can be reused across different maintenance‑order types. This ensures more consistent behavior across teams and scenarios. The translation of both the phase control code and its description have been streamlined as well, making the codes easier to understand and apply in multi‑language environments.

In addition, new analytics allow planners to analyze the throughput of backlogs based on the average time each backlog item takes to move through the maintenance phases. This enables better visibility of bottlenecks and helps identify root causes behind slowdowns or delays. The result is clearer insights into phase progression and improved control of the maintenance workflow. 

Phase control code and analytics based on phases

 

Master data and technical object innovations

Reuse value help app for technical objects

Finding the right technical object, whether equipment or a functional location, often meant navigating different value help dialogs with varying layouts and search behaviours. To simplify this experience, a new unified value help has been introduced across Asset Management. This enhancement provides a single, consistent, and intuitive way to search for equipment, functional locations, and other technical objects in Fiori apps.

The new Technical Object Reuse Value Help is built on RAP‑based CDS views and delivers a more efficient, flexible search. Users can choose from multiple search templates, each surfacing different fields depending on their task, for example, focusing on labels, maintenance data, sales information, or address details.

This not only speeds up how users find the right object but also brings a more modern and consistent user experience across apps such as Find Technical Object (F2072) Manage Technical Object BOMs (F6070) and Manage Serial Numbers (F7617).

Behind the scenes, the value help objects for equipment, technical objects, and functional locations are fully reusable, so any Fiori app can adopt them without redesign, ensuring a harmonized look and feel across the maintenance process.

Technical Object Reuse Value Help

 

Hierarchy structure view for reference structures

Managing consistent and scalable asset structures becomes easier with two major innovations: Reference Equipment and Reference Structures. Together, they introduce a more controlled and harmonized approach to defining master data structures across plants and sites.

Reference equipment serves as a master template for creating and governing individual equipment records. It centralizes key information, such as general data, classification, measuring points, partner data, and change history, and provides a consistent basis for downstream equipment creation and updates through the Manage Reference Equipment app (F8304).

Reference structures provide a hierarchical view of reference functional locations and reference equipment, displayed in an intuitive split‑screen layout that combines structure navigation with detailed object information. Using the Manage Reference Structures apps (F8311/F8669), organizations can build and maintain standard structural templates and use them as the master for mass creation of individual structures.

Together, these capabilities help enforce consistent asset modeling across sites, enable mass changes, and reduce manual effort and errors.

Manage Reference Equipment app (F8304)

Resource Scheduling enhancements (43R)

The 2025 FPS01 release delivers three improvements in Maintenance Resource Scheduling (43R), giving planners more flexibility when handling operations that are already underway and greater control over how utilization and scheduling logic behave across key apps.

Replanning for operations in process

Rescheduling and reassignment are now possible for operations with the status In Process, whereas previously only operations in Due or Dispatched status could be replanned. Planners can adjust times, change work centers, or reassign the work to different technicians directly from Resource Scheduling apps.

The enhancement applies to the following apps:

  • Manage Work Center Utilization (F2222)
  • Maintenance Scheduling Board (F2603)
  • Manage Maintenance Schedule for Assets (F4577)

To streamline the UI, the Info column has been removed from the Manage Work Center Utilization (F2222) and Maintenance Scheduling Board (F2603) apps.

Configuration for utilization calculation

A new configuration option allows key users to define how work should be distributed during utilization calculation for operations in In Process status.
Key users can now:

  • Decide whether remaining work or total planned work should be used
  • Define which date determines the start of the distribution (planned start date vs. start of current day)

These settings help organizations better represent actual workload and capacity during ongoing operations. The configuration is available in Plant Maintenance and Customer Service → Maintenance and Service Processing → Resource Scheduling → Make Settings for Resource Scheduling.

 

New BAdIs for Scheduling Logic (Manage Schedules – F3326)

Three new BAdIs give developers deep extensibility over how automatic and manual scheduling works in the Manage Schedules (F3326) app:

  • BADI_RSH_LEVELING_RULES – adjust rules used during scheduling
  • BADI_RSH_LEVELING_SEQUENCING – influence the sequence in which operations are processed
  • BADI_RSH_LEVELING_STRATEGY – fully replace the standard scheduling algorithm with a custom one

These BAdIs allow organizations to tailor scheduling behaviour to their own planning logic and capacity needs.

 

SAP Asset Performance Management 

SAP Asset Performance Management integrates with SAP Cloud ERP Private and empowers asset owners, plant managers, and reliability engineers to optimize maintenance strategies, measure and improve asset performance, and follow through on your sustainability objectives. The 2602 release includes improvements such as smarter notification replication between SAP ERP and APM, expanded industrial system modeling, extensible and time-aware FMEA assessments, and streamlined rule execution. Together, these updates help reduce manual effort while improving data clarity, structural transparency, and lifecycle control. Read the complete blog post by Kate Dowle: What's New in SAP Asset Performance Management 2602.

If you’d like to stay up to date with the latest APM innovations, follow my colleague Kate Dowle who regularly shares updates and new blog posts on the topic.

Summary

With these delivered innovations, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition FPS01 significantly strengthens Asset Management capabilities, from daily technician execution to long‑term planning and technical object governance. Each enhancement contributes to safer operations, improved data consistency, and more efficient maintenance processes, supporting organizations on their path toward intelligent, connected, and resilient asset performance.

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Also, visit the Asset Management community page. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to find our latest blog posts. 

Follow me on LinkedIn and stay up-to-date to our latest enablement assets: Fernanda Rodrigues | LinkedIn

More information

  • What’s New Viewer
  • From Innovation to Impact: SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 Release (SAP News Center)
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Community
  • Help Portal SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • What's New for Joule Capabilities
  • SAP Roadmap Explorer for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • SAP Business Suite Product Expert Training H1 2026

We at Cloud ERP Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: “Custom attributes” in ABAP platform workflows and related inboxes
entry.content:
SAP Business Workflow was first introduced in 1995 as part of SAP R/3 Release 3.0 (see: SAP R/3: Architecture, Function, Implementation" by Buck-Emden et.al..)

This longevity speaks to the robustness and deep integration of the tool in the ABAP platform – why also often the name ABAP platform workflow is used. A main goal was and is to distribute workitems (often also called “to dos” or “tasks” – don´t mix up this runtime artefact with the task-object from design time [e.g. TS30000016]) to the next worker to accomplish business outcomes in a coordinated way and as effortless as possible for human workers interacting with a digital system. Same is true for compliance approval processes implemented via flexible workflows in SAP S/4HANA CLOUD - we celebrate 10th Birthday this year.

Almost from the beginning, customizable task attributes and personalized worklist searching & sorting have been essential for enabling end-users to complete their work with freedom and flexibility—allowing them to work in the sequence their company or business process requires. This directly addresses all three psychological needs that Self-Determination Theory identifies as drivers of intrinsic motivation and self-actualization: autonomy, competence, and social relatedness. The concept of “custom attributes”: Company and business process-specific metadata that enables:

  • Task filtering and prioritization
  • Personalized worklist management
  • Context-aware task processing

This blog post deals mainly with the latest technology to define CDS Entity based custom attributes (which is not covered in the workflow overview training BIT602). For the sake of completeness, the older options are also mentioned or linked.

31 and also 10 years are tremendous for software components and tools. Congratulations to all contributed to that amazing story.

CDS Entity based custom attributes for My Inbox and SAP Task Center

CDS Entity-based custom attributes are the recommended approach for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and available sind ABAP Platform 2023. The CDS Entity must have identical key fields as the leading object associated with the workflow task or scenario. This method is supported for flexible workflow scenarios and their steps only.

Prerequisites Checklist

✓ Flexible workflow scenario with attached leading object

✓ CDS Entity key fields match leading object keys

✓ CDS Entity has @Workflow: { enabledFor: [ #CUSTOM_TASK_ATTRIBUTES ] } * key user possible [see section "Custom Attributes via key user extensibility" below]

✓ For amount/quantity fields: annotations include @Semantics

✓ Maximum 30 attributes per step (Task Center limitation)

Development

Note that it will only be possible to use the CDS based configuration for Tasks or Scenarios with an attached leading object.

At runtime, the system uses the CDS Entity name and field to populate custom attribute values. Configuration varies by deployment model:

  • SWF_USER_ATTR_DEF: Application development
  • SWF_USER_ATTR_CUST: Partner/OP customer development
  • SWF_USER_ATTR_CDS: Cloud customers (via SSCUI)

Note that the chosen CDS Entity has to have the annotation @Workflow: { enabledFor: [ #CUSTOM_TASK_ATTRIBUTES ] } if used in the transactions SWF_USER_ATTR_DEF, SWF_USER_ATTR_CUST or SWF_USER_ATTR_CDS. 

If the CDS entity field chosen is an amount or value field and carries one of the following annotations '@Semantics.amount.currencyCode' or '@Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure' the corresponding currency or unit information will be additionally exposed to the frontend. The label of the corresponding column would in this case be taken from the '@EndUserText.label' given in the CDS Entity'.

Note that the annotation referencing the currency or unit of measure is evaluated during definition of custom attribute. This means that if any changes are done to those annotations in the CDS view after the custom attribute was maintained these changes will not be evaluated anymore. If such changes are done the custom attribute should be removed and recreated.

Custom Attributes Definitions created in SWF_USER_ATTR_DEF are not displayed in the transaction SWF_USER_ATTR_CDS but can be redefined using the button.

Definition and customization

SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition provides three transactions for viewing, defining, and modifying CDS-based custom attributes:

Custom Attributes: Definition and Customization

Transaction Types

  1. SWF_USER_ATTR_DEF

 Purpose: Define custom attributes across all clients

  • Use Case: Typically used for solution delivery (e.g., by SAP Partners)
  • Transport Method: Workbench request
  1. SWF_USER_ATTR_CUST

Purpose: Define custom attributes for a specific client

  • Capabilities:
    • Define new custom attributes
    • Overwrite definitions from SWF_USER_ATTR_DEF
  • Transport Method: Customizing transport
  1. SWF_USER_ATTR_CDS

Purpose: Enable cloud customers to define or modify custom attributes

  • Availability:
    • SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition (full transaction access)
    • SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition (via SSCUI - CDS view definition only) available from 2402 onwards over "Configure Your Solution"
  • Key Features:
    • CDS-based definition capability
    • "Convert into CDS based cust. Attribute" button to migrate from task container definitions
    • "Copy Entry from System Settings" button to replicate existing definitions

Important Considerations

Aspect

Details

CDS Definition Restrictions

Only allowed if no task container definition exists

Conversion Impact

Converting from Expression to CDS removes the original definition and cannot be reverted by cloud customers

Already Redefined Attributes

Do not appear in the selection screen when copying

Maximum Limit

30 custom attributes per workflow step (Task Center service limitation)

Recommendation

Define maximum 30 custom attributes per step

Granularity Options

Task Level

  • Expose the same custom attributes across all workflow steps using that task
  • Example: Task TS78500059

Workflow Step Level

  • Expose custom attributes for a specific workflow step (identified by Step ID)
  • Example: Task WS02000006, Step ID 1
  • Benefit: Useful when a task is reused differently (e.g., different approval perspectives)

Configuration Fields

Field

Description

Notes

Use Case

Groups or filters user attributes

Free text (max. 20 characters)

Name of Attribute

Unique identifier

Special identifiers starting with "custom" display in specific inbox locations

Description

Identifying text

Max. 40 characters; translatable via ABAP translation mechanism

Rank

Priority/importance indicator

Higher rank = higher importance; affects display order in Task Center

CDS Entity

Source data entity name

(CDS-based only) Must have same key as the task's leading object

CDS Entity Field

Specific field containing display data

(CDS-based only)

Expression

Container element reference

(Container-based only) Use format: &ELEMENT_NAME&; supports combined values

Type

ABAP data type

(Container-based only)

Field

External field type

(Container-based only)

Screenshots

 

 

Custom Attributes via key user extensibility 

WIth SAP S/4HANA Cloud , public edition 2505 and 2025 private edition key users can define custom attributes through the SSCUI in the Manage Your Solution app. If additional CDS Views are needed as a basis for their definition, you can use App Custom CDS Views to generate a CDS View by selecting the Custom CDS Scenario "Custom Attributes for Task." See detailed documentation. Note that CDS views generated this way will appear in the value help within the SSCUI. However, you must ensure that the key fields of the generated CDS view match the key fields of the workflow task's or workflow scenario's leading object. Additionally, the transport containing the generated CDS view should be released either before or together with the transport containing the SSCUI configuration.

Special Attributes in Custom Attributes Configuration

This part explains how to create complex custom attributes (with relations available with ABAP Platform Cloud 2402 /ABAP Platform 2025) beyond simple transaction-maintained ones:

Three Types of Special Attributes

Type

Annotation

Use Case

Value + Unit of Measure

@Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure

Quantities with UOM (e.g., 10 KG)

Value + Currency

@Semantics.amount.currencyCode

Monetary amounts with currency (e.g., 100 USD)

Value + Description

@ObjectModel.text.element

Human-readable text pairs (e.g., Code + Description)

Please note, these related attributes are not available for My Inbox.

Key Requirements

CDS Entity Configuration

  • All referenced fields must be in the same CDS Entity, OR
  • Fields must be connected via an association with a single "on" condition

Text Arrangement (for Value + Description available from ABAP Platform Cloud 2405 / ABAP Platform 2025)

  • Use @ui.textArrangement: annotation to define display order
  • Default behavior: #TEXT_FIRST (description shown before value)

Language Considerations

Two Categories:

  1. Language Dependent
    • Example: itemCurrency, itemUOM
    • Evaluated per user's logon language
  2. Language Independent
    • Example: documentType, documentCategory
    • Same value across all languages

Language Management

  • Configuration Table: SWFCLANG (view: SWFCLANG_V)
  • User for Evaluation: SAP_WFRT logon language
  • Maintenance:
    • Private Cloud: SM30 transaction
    • Public Cloud: Configure Your Solution application
  • Storage: All texts stored with language keys per translation vector

 

Transport

Annotations and attribute configurations are transported together in the same transport request.

Sample CDS Entity and Custom Attributes

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED

@EndUserText.label: 'WF Demo: Leave Request Base (CDS)'

@ObjectModel.supportedCapabilities: [ #CDS_MODELING_ASSOCIATION_TARGET ]

@Workflow.enabledFor: [ #CUSTOM_TASK_ATTRIBUTES ]

define root view entity ZDK_WORKFLOWLEAVEREQUEST2
  as select from swxformabs

  association [0..1] to ZDK_DEMO_I_LR_TYPETEXT as _Text     on $projection.holidaytp1 = _Text.Type and _Text.Language = 'E'
  association [0..*] to I_Currency             as _Currency on $projection.Currency = _Currency.Currency

{
  key swxformabs.formnumber  as WorkflowLeaveRequest,

      swxformabs.approvby    as WFLeaveApprovedBy,
      swxformabs.createdby   as WFCreatedBy,

      swxformabs.addrnumber  as WFCreatorAddressNumber,
      swxformabs.pernr       as WFCreatorPersonalNumber,
      swxformabs.name        as WFCreatorNameText,
      swxformabs.department  as WFCreatorDepartment,
      swxformabs.costcenter  as WFCreatorCostcenter,

      swxformabs.firstday1   as WFLeavePartOneFirstDay,
      swxformabs.lastday1    as WFLeavePartOneLastDay,

      @ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: '_Text'
      @ObjectModel.text.element: [ '_Text.TypeText' ]
      swxformabs.holidaytp1,
      @EndUserText.label: 'LeaveRequest Type'
      _Text.TypeText         as LeaveType,
      swxformabs.reason      as WFLeaveReason,
      swxformabs.contact     as WFLeaveContact,
      swxformabs.procstate   as WFLeaveProcessState,

      case swxformabs.procstate
        when 'A' then 'Approved'
        when 'R' then 'Rejected'
        else 'New'
      end                    as WFLeaveProcessStateText,

      @EndUserText.label: 'LeaveRequest Currency'
      @ObjectModel.text.element: [ '_Currency._Text.CurrencyName' ]
      @UI.textArrangement: #TEXT_LAST
      case when swxformabs.holidaytp1 = 'R' then 'EUR'
           else 'USD'
      end                    as Currency,

      _Currency,
      _Text
}

 

Custom Attribute Types and Database Storage

Virtual Element-Based Custom Attributes

These attributes derive values from virtual CDS elements. The database record stores an exit-class name in the EXIT_CLASS field, which is used to calculate the attribute values.

Text Reference Custom Attributes

This configuration uses two database entries:

  • A primary custom attribute with REF_ATTRIBUTE_NAME pointing to a text reference attribute
  • A text reference custom attribute that provides the actual values
Currency-Based Custom Attributes

This structure requires three database entries arranged hierarchically:

  • Primary custom attribute (references a currency attribute)
  • Currency reference attribute (references a text attribute)
  • Text reference attribute (base values)
Unit of Measure Custom Attributes

Similar to currency attributes, this configuration also uses three entries:

Primary custom attribute (references a unit of measure attribute)

Unit of measure reference attribute (references a text attribute)

Text reference attribute (base values)

Implementation Considerations
Virtual CDS Elements

While virtual CDS elements are technically supported for custom attribute definitions, their use is not recommended for performance and maintenance reasons.

Runtime Behavior
Data Availability and Timing

Custom attribute values are calculated and stored when work items are created. However, not all container elements may be available at creation time, and CDS entity data retrieved at this point may differ from real-time values. Post-execution values and UI-updated attributes may not exist during creation, making them unsuitable for inbox display.

Dynamic Updates

Developers can update (available since ABAP Platform Cloud 2505 / ABAP Platform 2025) custom attributes post-creation using the CL_SWF_UTL_RUN_USR_ATTR_CHG utility class. After populating a local buffer with work items (via append_business_obj, append_workflow, or append_workitems), calling request_custom_attribute_chg asynchronously updates all non-final work items within that workflow

DATA(lo_util) = cl_swf_utl_run_usr_attr_chg=>get_instance( )
lo_util->append_workflow( '<workflow id>' ).
lo_util->request_custom_attribute_chg( ).

or business object.

DATA(lo_util) = cl_swf_utl_run_usr_attr_chg=>get_instance( ).

lo_util->append_business_obj( VALUE #( catid  = '<category_id>'
                                       typeid = '<type_id>'
