  • Troubleshoot service issues, capture header and payload data

      Internet Communication Manager Monitor

  • Transaction SMICM
  • Detailed HTTP calls (by increasing trace level to level 3)
  • Helps to identify authentication and SSL issues

       Refer to SAP Help: Gateway Tracing Tools

Still stuck? Refer to SAP Help: Joule - Monitoring and Troubleshooting which includes details on raising SAP incidents.

Troubleshooting:

  • Blog: Common configuration issues: Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • SAP Help: SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, standard edition - Troubleshooting
  • SAP Help: How to troubleshoot Cloud Connector principal propagation over HTTPS
  • SAP Help: Joule Constraints
  • SAP Note: 3649670 - Missing Joule icon in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition Standard Edition
  • SAP Note: 3666014 - Information required for Joule
  • SAP Note: 3673511 - Troubleshooting for Joule page display issues
  • SAP Note: 3679363 - How to recognize a Joule response's

Becoming an SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA guru

You’ll find much more on the community topic page for SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

Other helpful links in the SAP Community:

  • Follow our tag "S4HANA RIG" in "Enterprise Resource Planning Blog Posts by SAP" - SAP Community 
  • for more from the SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and RIG
  • See all questions and answers about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA
  • Follow SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA for more blogs and updates
  • Ask a Question about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: SAP Joule Copilot responses issue for BP or Sales process in S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition
entry.content:
Hi Experts/Gurus,

I have recently enabled Joule Copilot specifically joule base within S/4HANA Private Cloud edition 2023 version. It works fine for informational & navigational scenarios. However, when i am testing it for transactional capabilities like creating business partner or creating sales order with reference. It doesn't work as expected and it's hard to figure out what's the root cause as all standard roles & backend OData are active.

I am attaching screenshots as of now but i can give more details going forward if someone will engage on this post.
In sales order creation process, it asked for sales order type which i gave but then it stopped, no response at all but then i tried to ask simple information like when this sales order was created but it throws this error that its taking long time to get details from backend.
In BP process, it looks like there is no app for creating BP but i do have access of Manage Business Partner master data. So, hard to understand what went wrong here as well.
Joule SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 
@

entry.title: Object Release State in Cloudification Repository Viewer for Clean Core Extensibility
entry.content:
Introduction

The updated Clean Core Extensibility whitepaper was introduced in August 2025 by SAP as an evolution of the 3-Tier Extensibility concept. This updated the 3-Tier Extensibility to a more detailed assessment of classic ABAP extensions based on the clean core level concept, allowing you as SAP customers and partners stepwise transformation to clean core at your own pace .

While using external APIs in your ABAP custom code, one of the first things to check as per the updated level concept is the release state of that API in the Cloudification Repository Viewer. The release state tells you how stable the object is, what contractual guarantee SAP provides for it, and how your ATC checks will treat any usage of it. Getting this right is important because it directly affects your clean core compliance and your upgrade readiness.

SAP categorizes all objects in the Cloudification Repository Viewer into five distinct states. Each state carries a specific meaning for your custom code, and knowing the difference will help you make better decisions when selecting or replacing APIs.

The Five Release States in the Cloudification Repository Viewer

Here is what each state means in practice and what you should do when you encounter it.

Released

A Released API is one that SAP has formally published with a stability contract. Backward compatibility is guaranteed across upgrades, so you can build on these objects with confidence. Released APIs correspond to clean core Level A.

Level A objects are 100% Cloud compliant, 100% Clean core, can be achieved On-Stack, Side-by-Side or Hybrid.

If you are starting new development, Released APIs are your first choice. They represent the safest and most future-proof option available to you.

Deprecated

A Deprecated API was once Released but SAP is now phasing it out. It still functions today, but it has a planned end of life. SAP recommends that you do not start any new development on it.

If your existing code already uses a Deprecated object, that code is treated as clean core Level B and flagged as Priority 3 in ATC checks. Make sure to identify the successor API in the Cloudification Repository or the SAP Business Accelerator Hub and plan your migration. The sooner you move away from a Deprecated API, the less remediation work you will face when the object reaches its end-of-life date.

Classic API

Classic APIs are well-known, time-tested objects such as BAPIs and RFC-enabled function modules that SAP product experts nominated before a formal release process existed. They do not carry a formal stability contract, but they are upgrade stable and widely used across the SAP ecosystem.

Classic APIs correspond to clean core Level B and are flagged as Priority 3 in ATC checks. Keep in mind that while these objects are stable in practice, they do not carry the same forward-compatibility guarantee as Released APIs. Use them when no Released alternative is available and check the Cloudification Repository Viewer for the availability of the Released APIs as successors to Classic APIs.

Not to be Released

An API marked as Not to be Released is one that SAP has explicitly decided is not intended for external use. Any exemption for using such an object must include a migration plan toward a Released API.

These objects correspond to clean core Level C depending on the context and are flagged as Priority 2 in ATC checks. If you encounter this state, treat it as a signal to find a supported alternative as quickly as possible.

No API

An object classified as No API has no API contract whatsoever. Objects classified as noAPI must not be used, creates maximum technical debt.

This state is flagged as Priority 1 in ATC checks and must be addressed at the highest priority. If you find a noAPI object in your custom code, replacing it with a Released API must be your immediate next step.

Why Some APIs Are Listed as Both Released and Classic or Not to be Released and Classic

You may come across a scenario in the Cloudification Repository Viewer where the same API appears under both the Classic API and Released API categories or Not to be Released and Classic. This is a valid situation and worth understanding, because it can cause some confusion at first glance.

 

Take the CDS view I_EARMARKEDFUNDSDOCUMENTITEM as an example. If you check this object in the Cloudification Repository Viewer for the 2022 SP0 release of SAP Cloud ERP Private, it is listed only as a Classic API. When you switch to the 2025 FPS01 release, it appears as both Classic API and Released API.

 

 

What this tells you is that I_EARMARKEDFUNDSDOCUMENTITEM was formally Released in the 2025 FPS0 release of SAP Cloud ERP Private.

Both statuses are equally valid for this object. It carries the historical stability of a Classic API and now also has a formal stability contract as a Released API. In this case, the clean core status will be Level A as Released API will take the precedence and ATC check will not give any message.

Similarly, take the Class CL_HTTP_UTILITY as an example. If you check this object in the Cloudification Repository Viewer for the 2022 SP0 release of SAP Cloud ERP Private, it is listed only as a Classic API. When you switch to the 2025 FPS0 release, it appears as both Classic API and Not to be Released, with a successor.

Both statuses are equally valid for this object. It carries the historical stability of a Classic API and now also has a formal stability contract as a Not to be Released, with a successor.

In this case, the clean core status will be Level B as Classic API will take the precedence and ATC check will give a Priority 3 message. You are recommended to move to the successor API in this case.

This is why it is recommended to check the Cloudification Repository Viewer against the specific release you are working with.

Conclusion

The Cloudification Repository Viewer continues to grow with each release as SAP adds more objects and updates their states. Use it as your starting point before selecting any API in your custom code. Checking the release state upfront will save you significant remediation work during upgrade cycles and help you stay clean core compliant from day one.

We will continue to share updates on clean core extensibility as the tooling and API landscape evolves. In the meantime, refer to the Cloudification Repository Viewer and the SAP Business Accelerator Hub for the latest state of the objects relevant to your development.

entry.title: SAP Joule – Common Configuration Issues
entry.content:
Are you interested in activating Joule and integrating it seamlessly into your system—but running into errors along the way? In my role as solution architect for our SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and Regional Implementation Group I’ve worked with some of our first Joule customers and would like to share how you can avoid and resolve errors during the activation.

This blog highlights the most common SAP Joule issues encountered during provisioning, booster execution, and system integration.

You’ll also find clear, step-by-step guidance to help you quickly troubleshoot and succeed with your activation.
Please also refer to our SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and Joule Health Check blog to test your activation and configuration is working correctly before using Joule https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-build-work-zone-standard-edition-and-joule-health-checks/ba-p/14348321

We will look at the following topics:

  1. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) subaccount / provisioning issues
  2. Joule booster problems
  3. User Experience (UX) / icon appearance issues
  4. SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Provisioning service (IPS) and Identity Authentication service (IAS) issues
  5. Restrictions/ constraints
  6. Monitoring and troubleshooting
  7. References
  8. How to contact SAP Support

1. SAP BTP subaccount / provisioning issues

Issue

Solution/Available fix

You have a license for an SAP product that supports Joule integration (such as SAP SuccessFactors, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, etc.) and after creating a subaccount the Joule service is not available in the subaccount entitlement.

Check SAP Help:Data Centers Supported by Joule

The data centre is supported but the entitlements are not available in your global account.

Contact your SAP Account Manager/Executive. 

Refer to SAP Note 3639572 - Enabling Joule with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

SAP Help:Joule prerequisites

The menu entry "System Landscape" is not visible in the Global account of your SAP BTP Cockpit.

You are not an administrator of the Global account.

SAP Note 3456718 - Menu "System Landscape" is not visible in the Global account of SAP BTP Cockpit

SAP Note 2669325 - How to add a user as a SAP Business Technology Platform Global Account Administrator

SAP systems are not visible in SAP BTP under the system landscape.

 

 

Before adding your system, make sure it's not already auto-discovered and listed automatically on the System Landscape ---> Systems page.

Refer to SAP Help: BTP Registering SAP System
SAP Note 3661817 - SAP systems are missing in System Landscape of the BTP Cockpit

 2. Joule Activation/Issues during the execution of the Joule booster

While running the booster configuration for the Joule Service, the prerequisites check failed for the Identity Authentication Tenant.

 An error message is displayed: "Your global account does not have an Identity Authentication service tenant."

 

Ensure that the IAS tenant is visible under the global account and configure trust between the Identity Authentication Service (IAS) and the Joule subaccount. 

SAP Note 3675105 - Joule Setup Error: "Your global account does not have an identity authentication service tenant"

 

 

 

The Joule booster is being run, and you encounter the following error:

*********************************
Could not find SAP Cloud Identity system with host <IAS tenant>. 
Report an incident in the BC-CP-MP component 

The SAP Cloud Identity Services tenant isn't connected to the correct SAP BTP Global Account, or it's linked to the wrong customer ID.
How to Fix it:
a. Make sure your IAS tenant is linked to the correct Global Account.
Check the SAP Help:View Assigned Tenants and Admins.

b. If you have multiple IAS tenants , use the IAS tenant that matches the Global Account's customer ID.
Important:Data syncing between systems usually takes up to one week.Wait for this to complete before running the booster again so that the SAP IAS system appears in all the accounts.

You can manually trigger the sync via the “Refresh” button.
SAP Note 3620409 - Could not find SAP Cloud Identity system with host: Joule booster error

Enabling Joule leads to the following error: 
"Prerequisites for the formation type "Integration with Joule" are not fulfilled.
No SAP Cloud identity service system is showing up under“System landscape” --->System

 

A new SAP BTP Global Account will take some time to synchronize all existing customer systems, but you can manually trigger the sync via the "Refresh" button.
This should ensure that your SAP Cloud Identity Services system shows up. Afterwards you can proceed with the Joule configuration.

 

The SAP Build Work Zone system does not appear in the dropdown list during the setup process (Joule Booster - Step 5 Set Up Integrations).

 

 

The SAP Build Work Zone service was not subscribed to under the relevant subaccount.
An instance of this service is not enough for the integration; therefore, the system will not appear under the options.

Add the SAP Build Work Zone subscription in the Joule subaccount. Refer to
SAP Note 3710864 - SAP Build Work Zone system not visible in dropdown during setup

Unable to find the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition option in Step 3 - "Select Integration" stage dropdown during activation by means of the Joule Booster.

In global account Systems, the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition system is already auto-discovered.

Ensure that it is visible in Joule Booster Step 04  "Select Capabilities", select "S/4HANA Private Cloud" and proceed to complete the Joule booster.

Refer to SAP Note 3710921 - S/4HANA Private Cloud option is not listed in Step 3 - "Select Integration" in Joule Booster for detailed information.

Error message: "The subscription of subaccount <xxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxx> to application das-application and plan foundation, failed with state: SUBSCRIBE_FAILED."

Observe the error messages indicating SaaS Subscription failed and instructions to open a support case or unsubscribe and retry.

 

Refer to SAP Note 3689452 - Entity Saas Subscription failed after running Joule Booster for detailed instructions.

If the issue persists, raise a support incident and choose component CA-JOULE-PRV-ONB for further investigation.

 

 

 3. UX/ Icon appearance issue

Joule refused to frame 'https://xxx.xxx.cloud.sap/' because an ancestor violates the following Content Security Policy directive: "frame-ancestors”.                             

 

 

 

 

Enable 3rd party cookies of your browser.Add the trusted domain in SAP IAS and SAP BTP
a. Add trusted domain in SAP IAS in the SAP Cloud Identity Services tenant.
Go to Applications & Resources -->Tenant Settings --> Trusted Domains--> Click the + (Add) button
Type the URL of the trusted host into the field.

b. Add the trusted domain in SAP BTP.
In SAP BTP cockpit, navigate to your specific subaccount ---> Security ---> Settings.
Click the + (Add) button.
Enter the required hostname, for example: https://<your-s4-or-sfsf-url>.com

The Joule icon at the top app bar in the Fiori Launchpad is not properly displayed.

The issue is caused using an unsupported SAPUI5 version in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition system. 

 

Upgrade the SAPUI5 Component version of SAP S/4HANA to be equal to or higher than the minimum supported version.

SAP Notes:
2346367 - How to upgrade the SAPUI5 library in ABAP - SAP for Me
3641945 - Joule icon displayed wrongly in Fiori launchpad of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 
 

After integrating Joule with SAP Solutions, the user is unable to launch Joule by clicking the diamond icon,  it fails to load or respond.

 

 

Allow third-party cookies.

SAP Note 3643889 - User unable to launch the Joule Icon after integrating with SAP Solutions

The Joule icon is not appearing for a specific user despite that the correct role-based permissions have been assigned in SAP SuccessFactors.

The issue is most likely caused by security policies of the network provider, which may be blocking certain URLs that are required for the icon to appear.
The affected user does not have the General User Permission --->Access to Joule

Refer to SAP Note 3685621 - Joule button not appearing on SuccessFactors

After clicking on the Joule icon in SAP SuccessFactors, it tries to load for a few minutes and subsequently the user gets a blank or greyed-out screen.

Refer to SAP Note 3544482 - Getting Error / White /Blank / IAS login Screen When Trying to Launch Joule in SuccessFactors

4. SAP IPS/IAS issues

The SAP IAS source system read job is failing.

 

Keep the source system name the same as in the content channel that had been created in the standard SAP Build Work Zone.

Joule was successfully integrated with the SAP product. However, clicking the Joule icon results in a blank white or SAP IAS login screen. Single sign-on is not working.

 

Check the SAP IAS domain. In the SAP BTP Joule subaccount, go to Trust Configuration and select the Custom Identity Provider for Applications.

Change the field "Domain" to the one used in the SAP product.

Refer to SAP Note 3635949 - Blank white / IAS login screen appears when clicking on Joule icon from the solutions where Joule was integrated into

5. Restrictions /Constraints
SAP Joule operates within specific constraints.
SAP notes:
3698807 - How to Remove or Hide the Joule Icon in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
3711505 - Restrictions: Multiple different Work Zone editions integration with Joule
Before starting the Joule project, please refer to the document below. All constraints applicable to Joule are listed here.
SAP Help:Joule constraints

6. Monitoring and troubleshooting
You may experience issues that would have to be reported in SAP incidents. In such cases, please collect information, which will help the support team to investigate and resolve the problem quickly.
SAP Note 3666014 - Information required for Jouletroubleshooting
SAP Help:Monitoring and Troubleshooting

7. How to report issues
You can report an incident or error through SAP for Me. Ensure that you provide a detailed description of the issue including the steps to reproduce it. Attach any relevant screenshots, logs, or error messages to help with the investigation. Please use the appropriate components listed below when creating your incident.

General support component for core Joule services issue and functionality

CA_JOULE

Joule integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

CA-FLP-EXT-PCE-JOULE

SaaS Subscription error after running Joule Booster

CA-JOULE-PRV-ONB

8. Frequently asked questions

  1. Are there options available to customize the theme of Joule so it aligns with our branding guidelines and specific colour palette?

The theme tab is only available when Joule is used in a standalone (single page) mode. For now, Joule automatically adapts to the theme of the embedding application.  For example, if the user will changes the theme in the SAP Work Zone settings, Joule will automatically adopt it. The user cannot choose a different theme for the Joule client.
SAP Note 3646989 - Is it possible to adjust theming for Joule?

  1. Is it possible to remove the Joule icon for selected users?

When Joule is enabled through the Joule booster, it is activated for all users in both SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud systems. At present, there is no supported option to restrict or disable Joule for specific users or user groups.
SAP Note 3698807 - How to Remove or Hide the Joule Icon in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Summary
In this blog, we reviewed common configuration and activation issues related to Joule including challenges with SAP Business Technology Platform provisioning, Joule booster activation, UX visibility, and integration with SAP Cloud Identity Services (IPS/IAS), along with key restrictions, monitoring tips, and reference resources.

In addition, we discussed current system restrictions and limitations, monitoring options, log analysis techniques, and practical troubleshooting steps to help identify and resolve issues efficiently.

By following the guidance and best practices outlined above, administrators can streamline Joule activation, minimize configuration errors, and ensure a smoother user experience. If problems persist, contact SAP Support with detailed information such as error messages, screenshots, tenant details, and relevant logs to accelerate root cause analysis and resolution.

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and RIG team
